subDescription.accessibilityText: 8 months ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

text.text: This month, we welcome nearly 2,000 new interns from across the world, representing more than 400 colleges and universities. This talented group of individuals is joining us for a summer of professional development, hands-on experience, meaningful mentorship, and new connections – a testament to our commitment to investing in future talent. To the intern Class of 2025, welcome! Here's to an exciting few months ahead.

subDescription.accessibilityText: 6 months ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

text.text:
As the 2025 Bank of America Summer Internship Program comes to a close, I couldn’t be prouder of the impact our nearly 2,000 interns have made. Over the past 10 weeks, they’ve brought fresh perspectives, bold ideas and unstoppable energy to our teams - and in return, we’ve had the privilege of helping them grow their skills, networks and confidence. It has been an action-packed summer!

To the 2025 Bank of America summer interns, thank you for your curiosity, collaboration, and commitment. You’ve left your mark here! Wishing you all the best in your final months of university. You’ve got a bright future ahead!
#summerinternship
#greatplacetowork
#campusrecruitment

subDescription.accessibilityText: 10 months ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

text.text:
☎️ 📞Calling all Lawyers🤙🏽📲

LatinoJustice PRLDEF’s CAP Leadership Insititute is planning for our upcoming Summer 2025 LAWbound™ Program, our week-long pre-law bootcamp program designed to demystify law school and the admissions process while introducing program participants to experts in the field. Participating students will engage in opportunities to connect with law school students, attorneys, and judges, panel discussions and practical exercises such as mock law class, moot court, and personalized feedback on résumé/personal statement, and access to CAP Leadership Institute programs and resources.

We are currently recruiting attorney volunteers for a Writing Review Session on Personal Statements and Resumes that we are hosting at the tail end of the week on Friday, August 1, 2025, from 12:30-1:30pm EST. Attorney reviewers will be provided with their student's personal statement and resume at the start of the week so that they have ample time to review and prepare feedback, and then will meet with their assigned student during that hour-long writing review session on 8/1 to provide their personalized feedback.

We will be having students attending both in-person and remotely, so volunteers are free to join us in person at LJP Headquarters (475 Riverside Dr # 1901, New York, NY 10115) or remotely through Zoom. Any attorneys interested in joining for the session can reach out directly to Jonathan Reyes, Program Manager of the CAP Leadership Institute, at jreyes@latinojustice.org, and he can follow up with additional information about the session.

subDescription.accessibilityText: 2 days ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

text.text:
Calling all Ecuadorians in the tri-state area — we want to celebrate you. 🇪🇨
Raíces Ecuatorianas en América is hosting our Alumni Mixer on Wednesday, April 1st, and we'd love for you to be there. Bring your experience, your story, and your energy — our students are eager to meet the people who came before them and paved the way.

Come connect, share what you've learned, and see just how far our community has come. 

Rutgers Student Activities Center, New Brunswick, NJ 
April 1st | 7–9 PM

Can't make it in person? You can still be part of the night. Fill out the form below, and we'll include your info in a contact directory shared with every student at the event — so the connections happen with or without you in the room.

Alumni Mixer Interest Form:  https://lnkd.in/ej8cbj9v

description.text: Prev Finance Management Summer Analyst @BofA | ALPFA NJ | America Needs You Alumni
description.accessibilityText: Prev Finance Management Summer Analyst @BofA | ALPFA NJ | America Needs You Alumni

subDescription.accessibilityText: 6 months ago • Edited • Visible to Brayan’s connections

text.text:
This past Friday my role as a Finance Management Summer Analyst with Bank of America came to a close. The past 10 weeks were filled with constant learning and valuable exposure, where I learned about different teams in Global Markets finance. I was able to sharpen my technical skills while also learning how to be a part of a team critical for the financial management of the business. 

I’m incredibly grateful for all the invaluable lessons and insights I’ve gained in governance oversight, risk management, and financial markets. Thank you to all who made this an amazing experience. I’d like to recognize Jim Curran, Gabriella A. Ross, Andrea Zanetti Martins, and Luis Gasca for providing a stellar learning experience.  Special shoutouts to Nicole Sumpter, Martin Urquhart, Giovanni Nava, Zelda Wagner, Hemda Niknam, Priscilla Pacheco, and Steven Cheng for their insights and help this summer as well. I’m looking forward to carrying the skills I’ve gained onto the next chapter of my professional career. 

Lastly, very happy to have taken part in Bank of America FC. It was great being part of the team and seeing the amazing culture first- hand.

#BankofAmericaintern #FMAPSummerAnalyst

description.accessibilityText: Prev Finance Management Summer Analyst @BofA | ALPFA NJ | America Needs You Alumni

subDescription.accessibilityText: 10 months ago • Edited • Visible to Brayan’s connections

text.text:
I'm happy to share that I have accepted an offer to join Bank of America this summer as a Finance Management Summer Analyst in New York City! I'm incredibly excited to join the team and am deeply grateful for this opportunity.

Along the way, I've been fortunate to learn from incredible mentors and communities. Organizations like ALPFA Inc - Association of Latino Professionals For America and America Needs You, have played a pivotal role in my professional development. I'm especially thankful to Nestor Almeida, my mentor through ANY, Imani Reed, M.Ed., MBA, and Sofia Ruales, for their continued guidance and encouragement. A special thanks to Malik Vazquez and Arianna Collado as well. Lastly, I'd like to recognize Orlando Camargo, MS-RMI -- the insights you shared have stayed with me, and I truly appreciate the advice you shared at ALPFA convention last year. 

To everyone who's helped me get to this point -- thank you. I'm looking forward to the opportunities and growth ahead. 

#bankofamerica #bankofamericainternship #FMAPIntern

description.accessibilityText: Prev Finance Management Summer Analyst @BofA | ALPFA NJ | America Needs You Alumni

subDescription.accessibilityText: 1 year ago • Visible to Brayan’s connections

text.text:
As 2024 comes to an end, I wanted to express my gratitude and highlight the people and organizations that allowed me to channel a year of growth and excellence.  They have been pivotal to my personal and professional goals and I look forward to continue this onto 2025! 

⚖️ January 2024: Selected and completed the LatinoJustice PRLDEF LawBound Program! An incredible experience learning about law school admissions and working in the legal field. Special shout-out to Jonathan Reyes and the LJ team for creating an important program pre-law school.  

🏅May 2024: Graduated from America Needs You career development program! This organization holds a special place in my heart, as it was foundational to setting a framework for my academic and professional goals. Additionally, I showcased my public speaking skills being selected as a Fellow to speak at the annual Fundraising Party, which raised more than $80,000. Eternally grateful for all the mentors and staff that were a part of this experience in preparing many first generation students for their academic and professional goals. Special shoutout to my mentor Nestor Almeida; and Melisa Vallovera , Imani Reed, M.Ed., MBA , and John Cruz for their assistance and guidance!

🌟 June 2024: Began my internship with Meeting Essential Needs With Dignity. A stellar experience working in operations in the non profit sector as part of the Rutgers University–New Brunswick Scarlet Summer of Service Program! A special thanks to Christine Yoon for her mentorship this summer as I continued to tailor my skills and professional experience. 

📍August 2024: Attended my first ever ALPFA Inc - Association of Latino Professionals For America National Convention in Las Vegas, Nevada. A memorable experience networking and interviewing, where I ultimately landed a couple of offers! Incredibly thankful I was able to make this trip and for the help from colleagues and professionals. 

🎤 October and November 2024: Serving as Vice President of Raíces Ecuatorianas en America, also known as REA (the Ecuadorian Association at Rutgers), our eboard won best new organization award! We also hosted an Ecuadorian professional panel event for undergraduates looking for an opportunity to network and learn about the careers they pursue. 
 
🏆 December 2024: Adding onto last months success, REA organized Rutgers’ very first “Año-Viejo” cultural show. Through the eboards collective efforts, we produced a university-wide event that showcased the presence of the Latin American community at Rutgers! 

Additionally in 2024: Successful spring and fall semesters in 2024. Also accepted and continue to prepare for my 2025 Summer Internship!

subDescription.accessibilityText: 7 months ago • Visible to anyone on or off LinkedIn

text.text:
FIFA Club World Cup 2025™ recently concluded with Chelsea Football Club lifting the trophy after the final game against Paris Saint-Germain. 🏆 

During the 63-match tournament, nearly 2.5 million people showed up to cheer on their favorite teams – creating an electricity in the stands that was just as powerful as the action on the field. We’re proud to have been part of an event that delivered almost 6,400 incredible minutes of gameplay, while driving connection, community pride, and economic impact in host cities across the country.

FIFA World Cup 2026™ is just around the corner. As the Official Bank Sponsor of next year’s tournament, we’re continuing to deepen relationships in our communities as we celebrate this iconic sport on a global and local scale.
